|-------|-------------|-----------|
| (A) | **Domestic** corporation | §1361(b)(1)(A) |
| (B) | Not an "ineligible corporation" | §1361(b)(2) |
| (C) | ≤ **100 shareholders** (after §1361(c)(1) family attribution) | §1361(b)(1)(C) |
| (D) | Shareholders limited to individuals + qualifying estates + qualifying trusts + certain exempt orgs | §1361(b)(1)(D) |
| (E) | **No** nonresident alien shareholders (even ONE share terminates) | §1361(b)(1)(E) |
| (F) | Only **one class of stock** outstanding (voting-rights differences ARE permitted; economic differences are NOT) | §1361(b)(1)(F) |

**§1361(b)(2) ineligible corporation types** (4 categorical bars):
- Financial institutions using the §585 reserve method for bad debts
- Insurance companies subject to Subchapter L
- Foreign sales corporations (§922) and DISCs

**§1361(c)(1) family attribution** — treats "members of a family" as ONE shareholder for the 100-shareholder cap. Family = common ancestor + lineal descendants + spouses/former spouses of common ancestor or any descendant. A single family with 200 members + 50 unrelated shareholders = 1 + 50 = **51 effective shareholders**, well under the cap. Each family member must independently meet the permissible-shareholder requirements.

**§1361(c)(2) permitted trusts** — grantor trust, ESBT (electing small business trust), QSST (qualified subchapter S trust), voting trust, certain testamentary trusts. Other trust forms terminate the S election.

**Prong-failure priority ordering**: when multiple prongs fail simultaneously, the module reports the highest-priority failure first: (A) domestic → (B) ineligible corp type → (C) count → (D) type → (E) NRA → (F) classes. This matches the IRS audit-presentation order.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1361`. Twenty tests pin: **baseline qualifies** (50 effective shareholders, all 6 prongs pass); **foreign corp fails domestic prong**; **3 ineligible-corp-type failures** (financial institution reserve method + insurance Subchapter L + foreign sales corp / DISC); **exactly 100 shareholders qualifies** (boundary); **101 shareholders fails** (strict ≤ 100); **family attribution collapses 50 members to 1** (50 family + 49 non-family = 50 effective); **family attribution lets large family avoid cap** (200 family + 50 non-family = 51 effective); **partnership shareholder fails** §1361(b)(1)(D); **NRA shareholder fails** even one share; **two classes of stock fails**; **domestic failure short-circuits other failures** (regression — highest priority wins); **financial institution short-circuits count failure**; **count failure reported before NRA failure** (priority ordering); citation mentions all 6 prongs + §1361(c)(1) + §1361(c)(2); **citation mentions voting-rights differences ARE permitted** (regression — common confusion); note qualifying path mentions "qualifies as S corporation"; note failure path describes §1361(b)(1)(E) + nonresident alien; note reports effective shareholder count post-§1361(c)(1).

`traderview-expense::section_1367` is the **IRC § 1367 S-corp shareholder stock basis adjustment module** — core math for any trader organizing as an S corporation. § 1367 governs how shareholder stock basis shifts each year as the corporation's income, losses, distributions, and expenses flow through. The basis figure controls (a) how much corporate loss may be deducted on the personal return under § 1366(d), (b) whether distributions are tax-free under § 1368, and (c) gain recognition on disposition. Direct sibling to `section_1361` (S-corp definition + eligibility) and `section_1374` (built-in gains tax on C-to-S conversion). Form 7203 is the IRS basis-tracking schedule.

**§ 1367(a)(1) basis increases** (positive items, applied first):

| Subparagraph | Source | Item |
|--------------|--------|------|
| (a)(1)(A) | § 1366(a)(1)(A) | Separately stated income items (capital gains, charitable contribution pass-through) |
| (a)(1)(B) | § 1366(a)(1)(B) | Nonseparately computed income (ordinary trade-or-business income) |
| (a)(1)(C) | § 1367(a)(1)(C) | Excess of depletion deductions over basis of depleted property |

**§ 1367(a)(2) basis decreases** (negative items, floored at $0):

| Subparagraph | Source | Item |
|--------------|--------|------|
| (a)(2)(A) | § 1368 | Tax-free distributions |
| (a)(2)(B) | § 1366(a)(1)(A) | Separately stated loss/deduction items |
| (a)(2)(C) | § 1366(a)(1)(B) | Nonseparately computed loss |
| (a)(2)(D) | § 1367(a)(2)(D) | Noncapital, nondeductible expenses (50% meals under § 274(n), fines, lobbying, political contributions) |
| (a)(2)(E) | § 1367(a)(2)(E) | Shareholder depletion deduction |

**Treas. Reg. § 1.1367-1(f) — standard four-step ordering**:

| Step | Item | Excess treatment |
|------|------|------------------|
| 1 | INCREASES | n/a |
| 2 | DISTRIBUTIONS | Excess = capital gain under § 1368(b)(2) |
| 3 | NONDEDUCTIBLES | Excess **PERMANENTLY LOST** (no carryforward) |
| 4 | LOSSES | Excess **SUSPENDED** under § 1366(d)(2), carried forward indefinitely |

**Treas. Reg. § 1.1367-1(g) election — alternative ordering**:

| Step | Item | Excess treatment |
|------|------|------------------|
| 1 | INCREASES | n/a |
| 2 | DISTRIBUTIONS | Excess = capital gain under § 1368(b)(2) |
| 3 | LOSSES | Excess **SUSPENDED** under § 1366(d)(2) |
| 4 | NONDEDUCTIBLES | Excess **SUSPENDED + carried forward** under § 1.1367-1(g) (instead of lost) |

Election is BINDING in subsequent years unless IRS approves change back to standard rule. Election protects nondeductible-expense basis erosion at the cost of consuming more basis up-front for losses. Pinned by `election_changes_loss_allowed_when_basis_constrained` (same input under both rules produces different loss-allowed and nondeductible-applied amounts — standard order loses $10K nondeductibles permanently while election order saves them via carryforward; election order allows full $10K of losses while standard order allows $0).

**Standard ordering normal flow** — $100K beginning + $50K income = $150K → -$30K dist = $120K → -$20K nondeductible = $100K → -$40K loss = $60K ending. Pinned by `standard_ordering_normal_flow`.

**Loss suspension under § 1366(d)(2)** — $200K loss against $100K post-step-3 basis → $100K allowed + $100K suspended for indefinite carryforward. Pinned by `standard_loss_exceeds_basis_suspended`.

**Nondeductibles lost permanently under standard rule** — $30K nondeductibles against $20K basis → $20K applied + $10K LOST (no carryforward). Pinned by `standard_nondeductibles_exceed_basis_lost_permanently`.

**§ 1368(b)(2) excess distribution capital gain** — $50K distribution against $10K basis → $10K applied + $40K excess treated as gain from sale of stock (typically capital gain). Pinned by `standard_excess_distribution_capital_gain` and `distribution_above_basis_only_excess_is_gain`.

**§ 1.1367-1(g) election preserves nondeductibles** — $30K nondeductibles + $20K basis under election → $20K applied + $10K SUSPENDED for future carryforward (vs. LOST under standard). Pinned by `election_nondeductibles_suspend_when_exceed`.

**Multi-step ordering invariants** — `increases_applied_before_distributions_invariant` (income raises basis before distributions consume it), `distribution_applied_before_losses_invariant` (distributions step 2; losses step 4), `basis_never_goes_below_zero` ($0 floor at every step), and `standard_vs_election_same_ending_when_basis_unconstrained` (when basis covers all decreases, both orderings produce identical ending basis).

**Sibling S-corp cluster note** — every result references § 1361 (S-corp definition + eligibility), § 1366 (pass-through items + § 1366(d) basis-limited loss + § 1366(d)(2) carryforward), § 1368 (distribution mechanics — § 1368(b)(2) excess-distribution capital gain), § 1374 (built-in gains tax on C-to-S conversion), and Form 7203 (IRS basis-tracking schedule attached to shareholder's Form 1040). Pinned by `sibling_modules_note_present` (UX-text regression for 5-statute cluster + Form 7203).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1367`. Twenty-one tests pin: **standard ordering normal flow** ($100K + $50K - $30K - $20K - $40K = $60K); **standard loss exceeds basis suspended** ($200K loss → $100K allowed + $100K suspended); **standard nondeductibles exceed basis lost permanently** ($10K lost forever, no carryforward); **standard excess distribution capital gain** ($40K excess capital gain under § 1368(b)(2)); **election loss before nondeductible normal flow**; **election nondeductibles suspend when exceed** ($10K SUSPENDED under § 1.1367-1(g) instead of lost); **election changes loss allowed when basis constrained** (head-to-head comparison — standard $0 loss + $10K lost nondeductible; election $10K loss + $10K suspended nondeductible); **basis never goes below zero**; **increases applied before distributions invariant**; **distribution applied before losses invariant**; **distribution within basis no capital gain**; **distribution above basis only excess is gain**; **citation pins all subsections** (§ 1367 + § 1367(a)(1) + § 1367(a)(2) + § 1366(a)(1) + § 1366(d) + § 1366(d)(2) + § 1368 + § 1368(b)(2) + § 1.1367-1(f) + § 1.1367-1(g) + Form 7203); **sibling modules note present** (UX-text regression for § 1361 + § 1366 + § 1368 + § 1374 + Form 7203); **defensive negative beginning basis clamped**; **defensive negative income clamped**; **zero inputs zero ending basis**; **standard vs election same ending when basis unconstrained**; **separately stated combines with nonseparately income**; **separately stated loss combines with nonseparately loss**; **depletion deduction combines with nondeductibles for basis decrease**.

`traderview-expense::section_1368` is the **IRC § 1368 S-corp distribution module** — direct sibling to `section_1367` (basis adjustments). § 1368 governs SHAREHOLDER-LEVEL tax treatment of cash and property distributions from an S corporation. Result varies sharply depending on whether the S-corp has accumulated earnings and profits (E&P) from a prior C-corp period. Trader-critical for S-corps converted from C-corp status. Form 1120-S Schedule M-2 tracks AAA + E&P year over year.

**§ 1368(b) — no accumulated E&P** (S-corp never was C-corp, or E&P fully distributed). Two-step ordering:

| Step | Treatment |
|------|-----------|
| 1 | Tax-free reduction of stock basis (down to $0) |
| 2 | Excess = capital gain under § 1368(b)(2) (typically long-term) |

Pinned by `no_e_and_p_distribution_within_basis_all_tax_free`, `no_e_and_p_distribution_exceeds_basis_capital_gain` ($250K dist against $200K basis → $200K tax-free + $50K cap gain), and `no_e_and_p_zero_basis_full_capital_gain`.

**§ 1368(c) — with accumulated E&P** (default ordering). Four-step ordering:

| Step | Statute | Treatment | Basis effect |
|------|---------|-----------|--------------|
| 1 | § 1368(c)(1) | Tax-free up to AAA | Reduces basis under § 1367(a)(2)(A) |
| 2 | § 1368(c)(2) | Ordinary dividend up to E&P | NO basis reduction (taxed under § 301) |
| 3 | § 1368(c)(3) | Tax-free up to remaining basis | Reduces basis |
| 4 | § 1368(b)(2) | Capital gain on stock sale | n/a |

Pinned by `with_e_and_p_distribution_within_aaa_all_tax_free`, `with_e_and_p_aaa_exhausted_then_dividend` ($150K dist with $50K AAA + $80K E&P + $200K basis → $50K AAA + $80K dividend + $20K basis-tax-free), `with_e_and_p_exhausts_all_four_paths_capital_gain` ($1M dist with $50K AAA + $80K E&P + $100K basis → $50K + $80K + $50K + $820K capital gain), `dividend_never_reduces_basis_invariant` (E&P dividend leaves basis untouched), `boundary_distribution_exactly_aaa_no_dividend`, and `boundary_distribution_one_cent_above_aaa` ($1 to E&P dividend).

**§ 1368(e)(3) election — E&P first.** With unanimous consent of all shareholders to whom a distribution is made during the tax year, the S-corp may elect to REVERSE steps (c)(1) and (c)(2) — distribute E&P first as dividends, then AAA tax-free, then basis tax-free, then capital gain. Useful for purging E&P to avoid the § 1375 passive investment income tax or future § 1374 BIG tax exposure. Pinned by `election_e_and_p_first_reorders` ($100K dist with $30K AAA + $40K E&P → $40K dividend then $30K AAA then $30K basis under election; default would do $30K AAA then $40K dividend then $30K basis), and `election_ignored_when_no_e_and_p` (election has no effect when E&P = 0; falls through to § 1368(b)).

**§ 1368(e)(1)(C) net-negative-adjustment rule.** If for any taxable year the net negative adjustment exceeds the net positive adjustment, the AAA for purposes of § 1368(c)(1) is determined WITHOUT REGARD to such net negative adjustment — current-year-cushion for losses. AAA-available-for-distribution = beginning AAA + net positive (ignoring net negative when it exceeds positive). End-of-year AAA still reflects everything. Pinned by `net_negative_adjustment_ignored_for_distribution` ($100K beginning + $30K positive + $80K negative → AAA-available = $130K, all distribution from AAA; end-of-year AAA = -$30K after both adjustments + distribution), and `net_positive_exceeds_net_negative_both_apply` (when positive ≥ negative, both apply: $100K + $80K - $30K = $150K AAA-available).

**Negative AAA floored at $0 for distribution.** AAA may be negative due to prior-year losses, but distribution amount is limited to non-negative AAA. Pinned by `negative_aaa_floored_at_zero_for_distribution` (AAA -$100K with $80K E&P → 0 AAA used, $50K all from E&P dividend).

**Multi-regime invariants** — `dividend_only_when_e_and_p_present_invariant` (3×3 sweep — no dividend when E&P=0); `distribution_sum_equals_input_invariant` (3-cell sweep — total of all 4 categories equals distribution); `election_vs_default_when_e_and_p_present_different_ordering` (head-to-head election vs default for same input).

**Sibling S-corp cluster note** — every result references § 1361 (definition + eligibility), § 1366 (pass-through items + § 1366(d) basis-limited loss), § 1367 (basis adjustments — distributions reduce basis under § 1367(a)(2)(A)), § 1374 (built-in gains tax on C-to-S conversion), § 1375 (passive investment income tax — engages when S-corp has E&P AND >25% passive income), and Form 1120-S Schedule M-2. Pinned by `sibling_modules_note_present` (UX-text regression for 5-statute cluster + Form 1120-S Schedule M-2).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1368`. Twenty-three tests pin: **no E&P distribution within basis all tax-free**; **no E&P distribution exceeds basis capital gain** ($250K dist / $200K basis → $50K cap gain); **no E&P zero basis full capital gain**; **with E&P distribution within AAA all tax-free**; **with E&P AAA exhausted then dividend** ($50K AAA + $80K dividend + $20K basis = $150K); **with E&P exhausts all four paths capital gain** ($50K + $80K + $50K + $820K = $1M); **election E&P first reorders** ($40K dividend → $30K AAA → $30K basis under § 1368(e)(3)); **election ignored when no E&P** (falls through to § 1368(b)); **net negative adjustment ignored for distribution** (§ 1368(e)(1)(C)); **net positive exceeds net negative both apply** ($100K + $80K - $30K = $150K AAA); **negative AAA floored at zero for distribution**; **zero distribution no tax effects**; **dividend only when E&P present invariant** (3×3 sweep); **dividend never reduces basis invariant** ($5M dividend, basis unchanged); **distribution sum equals input invariant** (3-cell sweep); **election vs default when E&P present different ordering**; **citation pins all subsections** (§ 1368 + § 1368(a) + § 1368(b)/(b)(1)/(b)(2) + § 1368(c)/(c)(1)/(c)(2)/(c)(3) + § 1368(e)(1)(A)/(e)(1)(C)/(e)(3) + § 1.1368-1 + § 1.1368-2 + Form 1120-S Schedule M-2); **sibling modules note present** (UX-text regression for § 1361 + § 1366 + § 1367 + § 1374 + § 1375 + Form 1120-S Schedule M-2); **defensive negative distribution clamped**; **defensive negative basis clamped**; **defensive negative E&P clamped**; **boundary distribution exactly AAA no dividend**; **boundary distribution one cent above AAA** ($1 to E&P dividend).

`traderview-expense::section_1375` is the **IRC § 1375 S-corp passive investment income tax module** — completes the S-corp cluster after § 1361 (definition), § 1367 (basis adjustments, iter 246), § 1368 (distributions, iter 248), and § 1374 (built-in gains tax). § 1375 is the passive-investment-income tax — applies ONLY to S-corps with accumulated E&P from a prior C-corp period AND passive investment income exceeding 25% of gross receipts. Companion to § 1362(d)(3) three-year S-election termination rule with identical engagement triggers.

**§ 1375(a) engagement — TWO conditions, both required**:

| Condition | Authority | Trigger |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| (1) | § 1375(a)(1) | S-corp has accumulated E&P at close of taxable year (carried from prior C-corp period) |
| (2) | § 1375(a)(2) | Gross receipts more than 25% of which are passive investment income (§ 1362(d)(3)(C) — rents, royalties, dividends, interest, annuities) |

S-corps that never were C-corps have zero accumulated E&P and are NEVER subject to § 1375 regardless of passive income mix. Pinned by `no_e_and_p_no_tax_engagement` and `engagement_requires_both_triggers_truth_table` (4-cell truth table — only the {E&P=true, PII>25%=true} cell engages).

**§ 1375(b)(1)(B) Excess Net Passive Income (ENPI) formula**:

```
ENPI = NPI × (PII − 25% × GR) / PII
```

Where: PII = Passive Investment Income; GR = Gross Receipts; NPI = Net Passive Income (PII less directly connected deductions). Pinned by `enpi_baseline_math` ($1M GR + $500K PII + $400K NPI → ENPI = $400K × $250K / $500K = $200K → tax = $200K × 21% = $42K), `enpi_full_passive_income_when_pii_is_all_of_gross_receipts` ($800K NPI × $750K / $1M = $600K), and `enpi_formula_high_pii_low_npi` ($100K NPI × $550K / $800K = $68.75K).

**§ 1375(b)(1)(A) ENPI cap**: ENPI cannot exceed corporation's taxable income for the year. Preserves S-corp ability to survive even when entire passive income is "excess". Pinned by `enpi_capped_at_taxable_income` (TI cap below raw ENPI clamps tax accordingly) and `taxable_income_zero_caps_tax_at_zero` (zero taxable income → zero tax even when engaged).

**25% boundary precision** — the threshold uses exact-integer comparison `4 × PII > GR` rather than basis-point arithmetic, so the boundary is detected at single-cent precision. Pinned by `pii_at_25_percent_exactly_no_engagement` (exact 25% — not exceeded), `pii_just_above_25_percent_engages` (one cent above — exceeded), and `pii_below_25_percent_no_engagement`. Bp arithmetic is retained as display-only for the result struct's `pii_percentage_of_gross_receipts_bps`.

**§ 1362(d)(3) three-year S-election termination — companion provision**. Same engagement triggers as § 1375. Three consecutive years of E&P + >25% PII terminates the S election effective close of third taxable year; corporation reverts to C-corp under § 1362(d). § 1362(g) imposes 5-year waiting period before re-election unless IRS consents. Module reports warning levels at year 1 (first warning) and year 2 (elevated warning — one more year terminates), with full termination flag at year 3. Pinned by `consecutive_year_1_warning_level_1`, `consecutive_year_2_warning_level_2`, `consecutive_year_3_election_terminated`, and `consecutive_years_only_count_when_engagement_present` (no E&P → years counter irrelevant).

**Tax rate parameterization** — default `corporate_tax_rate_bps = 2100` (21% post-TCJA highest § 11(b) rate). Module accepts arbitrary rate for pre-TCJA or hypothetical analysis. Pinned by `pre_tcja_35_percent_rate` ($200K ENPI × 35% = $70K) and `zero_rate_no_tax` (rate = 0 → tax = 0 even when engaged).

**Multi-regime invariants** — `engagement_requires_both_triggers_truth_table` (4-cell sweep — only E&P+PII>25% engages); `pii_percentage_calculation_bps_invariant` (display-bps math reports 25%/30% correctly); `termination_threshold_constant_invariant` (2500 bps / 2100 bps / 3 years constants).

**Sibling S-corp cluster note** — every result references the full 5-module cluster: § 1361 (definition + eligibility), § 1366 (pass-through), § 1367 (basis), § 1368 (distributions — including § 1368(e)(3) election to distribute E&P first, which can purge accumulated E&P and avoid § 1375 + § 1362(d)(3) exposure), § 1374 (built-in gains tax — separate tax on C-to-S conversion gains, often coincides with § 1375 exposure for newly converted S-corps), and § 1362(d)(3) termination. Pinned by `sibling_modules_note_present` (UX-text regression for 5-statute cluster + § 1368(e)(3) election + § 1362(d)(3) termination).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1375`. Twenty-four tests pin: **no E&P no tax engagement**; **PII at 25% exactly no engagement** (boundary); **PII just above 25% engages** (one-cent precision via exact-integer comparison); **PII below 25% no engagement**; **ENPI baseline math** ($200K ENPI × 21% = $42K tax); **ENPI capped at taxable income**; **ENPI zero when no engagement**; **ENPI full passive income when PII is all of gross receipts** ($600K ENPI); **consecutive year 1 warning level 1**; **consecutive year 2 warning level 2**; **consecutive year 3 election terminated** (§ 1362(d)(3) violation); **consecutive years only count when engagement present** (no E&P → no termination risk); **pre-TCJA 35% rate** ($70K tax); **zero rate no tax** (engaged but tax = 0); **engagement requires both triggers truth table** (4-cell sweep); **PII percentage calculation BPS invariant** (display math); **termination threshold constant invariant** (2500 bps / 2100 bps / 3 years); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 1375 + § 1375(a)/(b)/(b)(1)(A)/(b)(1)(B)/(d) + § 11(b) + § 1362(d)(3)/(d)(3)(B)/(d)(3)(C) + § 1.1375-1 + § 1362(g)); **sibling modules note present** (UX-text regression for 5-statute cluster + § 1368(e)(3) + § 1362(d)(3)); **defensive negative inputs clamped**; **defensive zero gross receipts no overflow** (avoids division by zero); **defensive rate above 100% clamped**; **ENPI formula high PII low NPI** ($68.75K); **taxable income zero caps tax at zero**.

`traderview-expense::section_7345` is the **IRC § 7345 passport revocation for seriously delinquent tax debt module** — added by Section 32101 of the Fixing America's Surface Transportation (FAST) Act, Pub. L. 114-94 (December 4, 2015). Trader-critical for high-net-worth individuals with international travel needs and unresolved IRS tax issues. IRS certifies "seriously delinquent tax debt" (Notice CP508C) to the State Department, which then denies passport applications, revokes existing passports, or limits passport use. Sibling to the disclosure + penalty cluster: § 6011 (taxpayer disclosure), § 6651 (failure-to-file/pay penalties — source of much of the assessed amount), § 6654 (failure-to-pay-estimated-tax), § 6662 (accuracy penalty), § 6707A (taxpayer disclosure penalty). § 7345 is the COLLECTION pressure layer — operates after underlying tax liability has been assessed and collection action has begun.

**§ 7345(b)(1) "Seriously delinquent tax debt" — three required conditions**:

| Condition | Authority | Rule |
|-----------|-----------|------|
| (i) Threshold | § 7345(b)(1) | Unpaid federal tax liability (including penalties + interest) > inflation-adjusted threshold ($66,000 for 2025; $50,000 originally in 2015) |
| (ii) Collection action | § 7345(b)(1)(A) or (B) | EITHER (A) notice of federal tax lien under § 6323 filed AND § 6320 administrative remedies exhausted, OR (B) levy issued under § 6331 |
| (iii) No exclusion | § 7345(b)(2) | None of the eight § 7345(b)(2)(A)-(H) exclusions engaged |

All three required. Pinned by `baseline_seriously_delinquent_certification_engaged`, `debt_below_threshold_no_certification`, `debt_at_threshold_exactly_not_exceeding` (statute reads "exceeds", not ≥), `debt_one_cent_above_threshold_engages`, `no_lien_no_levy_no_engagement`, and `all_three_engagement_conditions_required_truth_table` (8-cell truth table — only the (above_threshold=true, collection=true, exclusion=false) cell engages).

**Collection action prong — lien-with-exhausted-remedies OR levy alone**. Notice of federal tax lien filed under § 6323 is necessary but NOT sufficient — § 6320 administrative remedies must also be exhausted or lapsed. Alternatively, levy issued under § 6331 alone satisfies the prong. Pinned by `lien_without_exhausted_remedies_no_engagement` (lien alone without administrative exhaustion does not engage) and `levy_alone_satisfies_collection_action` (no lien needed if levy issued).

**§ 7345(b)(2) eight statutory exclusions — each independently blocks certification**:

| Exclusion | Authority | Trigger |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| (A) | § 6159 | Installment agreement being timely paid |
| (B) | § 7122 | Offer in compromise accepted by IRS |
| (C) | § 6015 | Innocent spouse relief request pending |
| (D) | § 6320 / § 6330 | Collection due process hearing pending |
| (E) | n/a | Pending bankruptcy proceeding |
| (F) | n/a | IRS-identified identity theft victim |
| (G) | n/a | Federally declared disaster area |
| (H) | n/a | "Currently not collectible" status due to financial hardship |

Pinned by individual exclusion-blocks tests for all eight prongs plus `each_exclusion_independently_blocks_certification_invariant` (8-prong sweep — each exclusion independently disqualifies) and `multiple_exclusions_listed_individually` (multiple exclusions surface separately in result struct).

**§ 7345(c) reversal mechanics** — IRS notifies State Department within 30 days of any event resolving the debt (full payment, settlement, exclusion engagement, certification found erroneous). Notice CP508R issued to taxpayer. Reversal notification window pinned via `REVERSAL_NOTIFICATION_DAYS = 30` constant.

**Threshold inflation adjustment** — module accepts a parameterized `annual_threshold_cents` to support any tax year. 2025 constant is $66,000 = 6,600,000 cents; 2015 original was $50,000 = 5,000,000 cents. Pinned by `threshold_constants_invariant` (2025 > 2015; 30-day reversal window) and `parameterized_threshold_2024_value` (2024 threshold = $62K, confirms parameterization works).

**§ 7345(e) judicial review** — taxpayer may seek review in U.S. Tax Court OR U.S. District Court for the district where the taxpayer resides. Citation includes this authority for users contesting certification.

**Sibling cluster note** — every result references the disclosure + penalty siblings (§ 6011, § 6651, § 6654, § 6662, § 6707A) and identifies § 7345 as the COLLECTION pressure layer that engages after assessment + collection action. FAST Act § 32101 origin cited. Pinned by `sibling_modules_note_present` (UX-text regression for 5-statute cluster + FAST Act origin).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7345`. Twenty-five tests pin: **baseline seriously delinquent certification engaged**; **debt below threshold no certification**; **debt at threshold exactly not exceeding** (statute reads "exceeds"); **debt one cent above threshold engages**; **lien without exhausted remedies no engagement**; **levy alone satisfies collection action**; **no lien no levy no engagement**; eight individual exclusion-blocks tests (installment agreement, OIC, innocent spouse, CDP, bankruptcy, identity theft, disaster area, CNC); **multiple exclusions listed individually** (3 simultaneous exclusions); **all three engagement conditions required truth table** (8-cell sweep); **each exclusion independently blocks certification invariant** (8-prong sweep); **threshold constants invariant** ($66K 2025 > $50K 2015; 30-day reversal); **parameterized threshold 2024 value** ($62K — confirms inflation-adjustment parameterization); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 7345 + § 7345(b)(1) + § 7345(b)(1)(A)/(B) + § 7345(b)(2) + § 7345(c)/(d)/(e) + FAST Act § 32101 + December 4, 2015 + § 6159 + § 7122 + § 6015 + § 6320 + § 6330 + § 6323 + § 6331 + CP508C + CP508R); **sibling modules note present** (UX-text regression for § 6011 + § 6651 + § 6654 + § 6662 + § 6707A + FAST Act origin); **defensive negative debt no engagement**; **defensive negative threshold clamped to zero**; **zero debt no engagement**.

`traderview-expense::section_7408` is the **IRC § 7408 injunction-remedy module** — completes the preparer-and-promoter enforcement cluster (§ 6694 + § 6695 + § 6700 + § 6701 + § 7408). § 7408 is the EQUITABLE INJUNCTION authority IRS uses to STOP ongoing promoter/aider conduct, distinct from the civil penalty assessments under § 6700/§ 6701. § 7408 supplements (does not replace) the penalty sections; IRS may pursue penalties and injunction in parallel under § 7402(a) independent-jurisdiction rule.

**§ 7408(b) two-prong injunction test** — court may issue injunction when BOTH satisfied:

| Prong | Statute | Test |
|-------|---------|------|
| (1) | § 7408(b)(1) | Person engaged in specified conduct (any of 5 enumerated categories) |
| (2) | § 7408(b)(2) | Injunctive relief appropriate to PREVENT RECURRENCE |

Plus § 7408(a) Secretary-request requirement.

**§ 7408(c) five specified-conduct categories**:

| Category | Statute |
|----------|---------|
| § 6700 abusive tax shelter promotion | § 7408(c) |
| § 6701 aiding and abetting understatement | § 7408(c) |
| § 6707 material advisor failure-to-furnish | § 7408(c) |
| § 6708 material advisor list-maintenance failure | § 7408(c) |
| Circular 230 violation | 31 U.S.C. § 330 regulations |

**§ 7408(d) venue** — district court for the district where person resides, has principal place of business, or has engaged in specified conduct.

**§ 7408(e) non-resident venue rule** — U.S. citizen/resident not residing in any U.S. judicial district treated as residing in DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA. Pinned by `non_resident_treated_as_dc_venue` and `resident_uses_normal_venue`.

**Three-element gating** — injunction requires (1) Secretary request, (2) specified conduct, (3) appropriateness to prevent recurrence. Missing any element defeats the action. Pinned by `no_secretary_request_no_injunction`, `no_specified_conduct_no_injunction`, `no_appropriateness_no_injunction`, and `all_three_elements_engages_injunction`.

**Five specified-conduct categories each independently engage** — § 6700, § 6701, § 6707, § 6708, Circular 230. Pinned by `section_6700_conduct_engages`, `section_6701_conduct_engages`, `section_6707_conduct_engages`, `section_6708_conduct_engages`, `circular_230_violation_engages`, `each_conduct_category_independently_engages_invariant` (5-prong sweep), and `all_five_categories_engaged` (combined).

**§ 7402(a) supplemental jurisdiction** — § 7408 action proceeds SEPARATELY and APART from any other government action. Injunction does NOT preclude (1) civil penalty assessment under § 6700/§ 6701/§ 6707/§ 6708, (2) criminal prosecution under § 7201/§ 7203/§ 7206 + 18 U.S.C. § 371 conspiracy, (3) Circular 230 disciplinary proceedings, or (4) other equitable remedies. § 7408 is SUPPLEMENTAL — IRS may pursue parallel tracks. Pinned by `supplemental_jurisdiction_note_present`.

**Multi-element truth table** — `three_required_elements_truth_table` (8-cell sweep over Secretary × conduct × appropriateness; only all-three-true engages); `each_conduct_category_independently_engages_invariant` (5-prong sweep — each specified conduct alone satisfies § 7408(b)(1)); `venue_dc_only_when_not_us_resident_invariant` (2-cell sweep).

**Sibling cluster note** — every result references the full preparer-and-promoter cluster (§ 6694 + § 6695 + § 6700 + § 6701) plus material-advisor cluster (§ 6707 + § 6708) plus § 7407 parallel preparer-specific injunction provision plus EQUITABLE REMEDY distinction from penalty assessments. Pinned by `sibling_cluster_note_present` (UX-text regression for full enforcement cluster + § 7407 parallel + equitable-remedy distinction).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7408`. Twenty-two tests pin: **no Secretary request no injunction**; **no specified conduct no injunction**; **no appropriateness no injunction**; **all three elements engages injunction**; **§ 6700 conduct engages**; **§ 6701 conduct engages**; **§ 6707 conduct engages**; **§ 6708 conduct engages**; **Circular 230 violation engages**; **multiple categories listed individually** (3 simultaneous); **all five categories engaged**; **non-resident treated as DC venue** (§ 7408(e)); **resident uses normal venue**; **three required elements truth table** (8-cell sweep — only all-three-true engages); **each conduct category independently engages invariant** (5-prong sweep); **venue DC only when not US resident invariant**; **citation pins all subsections** (§ 7408 + (a)/(b)/(b)(1)/(b)(2)/(c)/(c)(1)-(5)/(d)/(e) + § 7402(a) + § 6700 + § 6701 + § 6707 + § 6708 + 31 U.S.C. § 330 + § 7407 + IRM 5.20.7 + IRM 5.20.8); **sibling cluster note present** (UX-text regression for § 6694 + § 6695 + § 6700 + § 6701 + § 6707 + § 6708 + § 7407 + EQUITABLE REMEDY distinction); **supplemental jurisdiction note present** (UX-text regression for § 7402(a) + parallel-track availability + criminal prosecution + Circular 230); **no Secretary request specific note**; **no conduct specific note**; **no appropriateness specific note**.

`traderview-expense::section_721` is the **IRC § 721 partnership contribution non-recognition module** — partnership-side counterpart to § 351 (corporate incorporation). Trader-critical for hedge fund formation, real estate JVs, fund-of-fund structures, and any partnership-based investment vehicle. § 721(a) provides BILATERAL non-recognition (both partnership and partners) on contribution of property in exchange for partnership interest — broader than § 351 which is one-way.

**Four-subsection structure**:

| Subsection | Rule | Trader-relevance |
|------------|------|------------------|
| § 721(a) | General non-recognition on contribution | Most common path |
| § 721(b) | **Investment company exception** — > 80% readily marketable stocks/securities triggers gain recognition | **Hedge fund formation trap** — prevents tax-free diversification |
| § 721(c) | **Related foreign partner gain recognition** (effective Jan 18, 2017) | Cross-border partnership formation |
| § 721(d) | Recapture rules tie-in with § 736(a) retiring partner distribution | End-of-life partnership transactions |

**§ 721(a) general non-recognition — applies symmetrically to gain AND loss**. No recognition by partnership or partners. Built-in gain/loss deferred until partnership disposes of the property or partner disposes of partnership interest. § 704(c) governs eventual allocation upon disposition. Pinned by `baseline_section_721a_no_recognition`, `loss_property_no_recognition` (loss treated symmetrically), and `zero_basis_full_built_in_gain_no_recognition` ($100K gain still deferred).

**§ 721(b) investment company exception — applies to GAIN only**. If partnership would be treated as investment company under § 351 (> 80% of partnership's assets held for investment AND consist of readily marketable stocks or securities), built-in gain is recognized immediately. Designed to prevent tax-free diversification of concentrated portfolios. Losses retain § 721(a) non-recognition treatment under § 721(b). Pinned by `investment_company_exception_engages_recognition` ($60K gain recognized) and `investment_company_loss_still_disallowed` (loss continues to be non-recognized).

**§ 721(c) related foreign partner gain recognition — effective January 18, 2017**. Treas. Reg. § 1.721(c)-2 imposed gain recognition when (a) partnership formed on/after January 18, 2017 AND (b) related foreign person is a partner (under § 267(b) or § 707(b)(1)) AND (c) property has built-in gain (FMV > basis). Pinned by `section_721c_engages_post_2017_no_gdm`, `section_721c_pre_2017_no_recognition` (effective date grandfather), `section_721c_no_foreign_partner_no_recognition`, and `section_721c_loss_property_no_recognition` (gain-only application).

**§ 1.721(c)-3 Gain Deferral Method (GDM) safe harbor**. If partnership adopts GDM, U.S. transferor defers built-in gain and recognizes it ratably over the property's recovery period via remedial income allocations. Acceleration events (reduction in U.S. transferor's interest, disposition of § 721(c) property) trigger remaining gain recognition. Pinned by `section_721c_gdm_defers_gain` (full $60K deferred under GDM) and `gdm_only_engages_with_section_721c_invariant` (GDM adoption meaningful only when § 721(c) engages).

**§ 721(d) recapture — tied to § 736(a)**. When partnership interest is distributed in liquidation of a retiring partner under § 736(a), built-in gain inherent in the partnership interest may be recharacterized as ordinary income under recapture provisions. Pinned by `section_721d_recapture_note_present`.

**Multi-regime invariants** — `section_721a_applies_when_no_exception_engaged_invariant` (4-cell sweep — § 721(a) applies only when neither (b) nor (c) overrides); `gain_only_recognized_on_built_in_gain_property_invariant` (both § 721(b) and § 721(c) apply only to gain; losses retain § 721(a) treatment in both exception paths); `gdm_only_engages_with_section_721c_invariant` (GDM adoption meaningful only when § 721(c) engages); `realized_gain_loss_arithmetic_invariant` (4-cell FMV × basis sweep — gain, loss, no-gain-no-loss, zero-FMV-full-loss).

**Combined § 721(b) + § 721(c) engagement** — both exceptions may engage simultaneously when partnership is both investment company AND has related foreign partner formed post-2017. Module reports both for transparency (in practice IRS picks one). Pinned by `both_exceptions_engage_total_gain_aggregates`.

**Sibling partnership tax cluster note** — every result references § 351 (corporate-side counterpart — one-way vs. § 721's bilateral), § 704(c) (built-in gain allocation upon eventual disposition — companion module because deferred gain is tracked and allocated under § 704(c)), § 752 (partnership liabilities — partner's share affects outside basis), § 754 (basis adjustment election — inside-outside basis alignment), § 267(b) + § 707(b)(1) (related person definitions used by § 721(c)), and trader-relevance for hedge fund / real estate JV / fund-of-fund structures. Pinned by `sibling_cluster_note_present` (UX-text regression for full partnership cluster + trader-relevance).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-721`. Twenty-one tests pin: **baseline § 721(a) no recognition** ($60K built-in gain deferred); **loss property no recognition** (symmetric treatment under § 721(a)); **zero basis full built-in gain no recognition**; **investment company exception engages recognition** ($60K gain recognized under § 721(b)); **investment company loss still disallowed** (§ 721(b) gain-only application); **§ 721(c) engages post-2017 no GDM** ($60K gain recognized); **§ 721(c) GDM defers gain** (full deferral under § 1.721(c)-3); **§ 721(c) pre-2017 no recognition** (effective date grandfather); **§ 721(c) no foreign partner no recognition**; **§ 721(c) loss property no recognition** (gain-only); **both exceptions engage total gain aggregates** (transparency reporting); **§ 721(a) applies when no exception engaged invariant** (4-cell sweep); **gain only recognized on built-in gain property invariant** (losses retain § 721(a) under both exception paths); **GDM only engages with § 721(c) invariant**; **realized gain/loss arithmetic invariant** (4-cell FMV × basis sweep); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 721 + (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) + § 1.721(c)-2 + § 1.721(c)-3 + § 1.351-1(c)(1) + § 351 + § 267(b) + § 707(b)(1) + § 704(c) + § 752 + § 754 + § 736 + Form 8865 + Jan 18, 2017); **sibling cluster note present** (UX-text regression for § 351 + § 704(c) + § 752 + § 754 + related-person + trader-relevance); **§ 721(d) recapture note present** (§ 736(a) tie-in); **defensive negative FMV clamped**; **defensive negative basis clamped**; **extreme amounts no overflow** ($1B FMV / $10M basis → $990M gain via saturating math).

`traderview-expense::section_731` is the **IRC § 731 partnership distribution gain/loss recognition module** — direct sibling to `section_721` (contribution non-recognition, iter 264) — completes the partnership contribution/distribution cycle. Trader-critical for hedge fund redemptions, real estate JV exits, fund-of-fund distributions, and any partnership-based investment vehicle's exit math.

**Four-subsection structure**:

| Subsection | Rule | Application |
|------------|------|-------------|
| § 731(a)(1) | GAIN — only to extent money distributed exceeds partner's outside basis | Current AND liquidating distributions |
| § 731(a)(2) | LOSS — only on LIQUIDATING distribution when partner receives only money + § 751 hot assets + inventory | Liquidating only |
| § 731(b) | Partnership-level NO RECOGNITION on distribution | Always — partnership entity is non-event |
| § 731(c) | MARKETABLE SECURITIES treated as money for gain calculation | Default; § 731(c)(3) exceptions |

**§ 731(a)(1) gain recognition** — money distributed exceeds partner's outside basis → recognized as gain under § 741 (typically capital gain). Applies to both current and liquidating distributions. Pinned by `current_money_within_basis_no_gain`, `current_money_exceeds_basis_gain_recognized` ($150K money - $100K basis = $50K gain), `liquidating_money_exceeds_basis_gain_recognized`, `money_exactly_equals_basis_no_gain` (boundary), and `current_distribution_truth_table` (4-cell sweep over money × basis combinations).

**§ 731(a)(2) loss recognition — strictest preconditions**. LIQUIDATING only AND partner receives nothing but money + § 751 hot assets + inventory. Receipt of ANY other property (real estate, equipment, intangibles) disables loss recognition entirely; built-in loss carries into basis of distributed property under § 732. Pinned by `current_distribution_no_loss_even_below_basis` (current distribution can never trigger loss), `liquidating_money_below_basis_loss_recognized` ($60K money + $100K basis → $40K loss), `liquidating_other_property_received_no_loss` (other property disables loss), `liquidating_with_hot_assets_inventory_loss_reduced` (loss reduced by basis in hot assets + inventory: $100K - ($40K money + $20K hot + $10K inventory) = $30K loss), and `liquidating_total_received_exceeds_basis_no_loss` (when total exceeds basis, gain not loss).

**§ 731(b) partnership-level non-recognition — always engaged**. No gain or loss recognized to the partnership entity on any distribution. Partnership distributions are non-events for the partnership; § 754 election + § 743(b) govern subsequent inside/outside basis interactions. Pinned by `partnership_level_no_recognition_always` (multi-scenario sweep) and `partnership_level_b_note_present` (UX-text regression for § 754 + § 743(b) cross-references).

**§ 731(c) marketable securities — treated as money for gain calculation**. Designed to prevent partnerships from converting appreciated securities into partnership interests and then distributing the securities without gain recognition. § 731(c)(3) exceptions: (a) distribution by investment partnership; (b) contribution-rollover safe harbor (same securities contributed by same partner returned); (c) reduction-of-net-gain rule preserving partner's pro-rata share of unrealized gain. Treas. Reg. § 1.731-2 implementation. Pinned by `marketable_securities_treated_as_money_default`, `marketable_securities_exception_not_money` (§ 731(c)(3) safe harbor), `cash_plus_marketable_securities_combined` ($50K cash + $80K securities = $130K money treated; $30K gain on $100K basis), and `section_731c_treated_as_money_only_when_no_exception_invariant` (2-cell sweep).

**Multi-regime invariants** — `gain_never_negative_invariant` (5-cell money sweep — gain always ≥ 0 via saturating clamp); `loss_recognition_only_on_liquidating_invariant` (current vs liquidating with same low-basis path); `loss_recognition_disabled_by_other_property_invariant` (2-cell other-property sweep); `section_731c_treated_as_money_only_when_no_exception_invariant` (2-cell exception sweep); `current_distribution_truth_table` (4-cell money × basis combinations).

**Sibling partnership cluster note** — every result references § 721 (contribution non-recognition — iter 264 companion module), § 732 (basis of distributed property — when basis carries from inside vs outside), § 733 (reduction of partner's basis upon distribution), § 736 (retiring/deceased partner distributions — § 736(a) payments characterized as distributive share), § 751 (unrealized receivables + inventory — "hot assets" referenced in § 731(a)(2) loss path), § 754 (basis adjustment election), § 707(c) (guaranteed payments — distinct from § 731 distributions), and trader-relevance for hedge fund redemptions / real estate JV exits / fund-of-fund distributions. Pinned by `sibling_cluster_note_present` (UX-text regression for partnership cluster + trader-relevance).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-731`. Twenty-four tests pin: **current money within basis no gain**; **current money exceeds basis gain recognized** ($50K gain); **liquidating money exceeds basis gain recognized**; **money exactly equals basis no gain** (boundary); **current distribution no loss even below basis** (current never triggers loss); **liquidating money below basis loss recognized** ($40K loss); **liquidating other property received no loss** (other property disables loss + § 732 carryover note); **liquidating with hot assets + inventory loss reduced** ($30K reduced loss); **liquidating total received exceeds basis no loss** (gain not loss); **marketable securities treated as money default**; **marketable securities exception not money**; **cash plus marketable securities combined** ($130K combined → $30K gain); **partnership-level no recognition always** (multi-scenario sweep); **gain never negative invariant** (5-cell sweep); **loss recognition only on liquidating invariant**; **loss recognition disabled by other property invariant** (2-cell sweep); **§ 731(c) treated as money only when no exception invariant** (2-cell sweep); **current distribution truth table** (4-cell money × basis sweep); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 731 + (a)(1)/(a)(2)/(b)/(c)/(c)(3)/(d) + § 1.731-1 + § 1.731-2 + § 721 + § 732 + § 733 + § 736 + § 751 + § 754 + § 707(c)); **sibling cluster note present** (UX-text regression for partnership cluster + trader-relevance); **partnership-level (b) note present** (§ 731(b) + § 754 + § 743(b) UX-text regression); **defensive negative basis clamped**; **defensive negative money clamped**; **extreme amounts no overflow** ($1B money / $10M basis → $990M gain via saturating math).

`traderview-expense::section_752` is the **IRC § 752 partnership liabilities module** — completes the partnership-tax cluster after § 721 (contribution non-recognition, iter 264) and § 731 (distribution gain/loss, iter 266). § 752 is STRUCTURALLY CENTRAL to partnership taxation because partner's share of partnership liabilities is treated as MONEY CONTRIBUTED OR DISTRIBUTED for outside basis purposes — directly affects § 731 distribution gain/loss math and § 704(d) basis-limited loss deductions. Trader-critical for real estate partnerships with mortgage debt, hedge funds with margin debt, and any leveraged partnership structure.

**§ 752(a)/(b) basis-flow rule**:

| Subsection | Trigger | Treatment | Effect |
|------------|---------|-----------|--------|
| § 752(a) | Partner's share of liabilities INCREASES | Deemed MONEY CONTRIBUTION | Outside basis ↑ dollar-for-dollar |
| § 752(b) | Partner's share of liabilities DECREASES | Deemed MONEY DISTRIBUTION | Outside basis ↓; may trigger § 731(a)(1) gain |

§ 752(c) — liability to which property is subject is treated as a partnership liability (and considered nonrecourse) to extent of property's FMV. Treas. Reg. § 1.752-1 NETTING RULE — single transaction with both increases and decreases collapses to net change.

**§ 731(a)(1) gain interaction**. Deemed distribution under § 752(b) feeds § 731(a)(1) gain math; gain triggered when deemed distribution exceeds partner's outside basis. Trader-critical: refinancing or assumption events can trigger gain WITHOUT any actual cash distribution. Pinned by `liability_decrease_exceeds_basis_triggers_731_gain` ($100K decrease, $50K basis → $50K § 731 gain), `liability_decrease_exactly_equals_basis_no_gain` (boundary), and `section_731_gain_only_when_distribution_exceeds_basis_invariant` (4-cell sweep).

**§ 1.752-2 recourse allocation — economic risk of loss (EROL)**. Partner who bears EROL (constructive liquidation analysis) receives full share of the recourse liability. Final Regulations TD 10014 (December 2, 2024) clarified multi-partner risk-sharing, tiered partnerships, related-party rules. Pinned by `recourse_allocation_when_bears_erol`.

**§ 1.752-3 nonrecourse three-tier allocation** (when no EROL):

| Tier | Source | Authority |
|------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | Partner's share of partnership minimum gain | § 704(b) + Treas. Reg. § 1.704-2 |
| 2 | § 704(c) gain on hypothetical disposition for full debt satisfaction | § 704(c) + Treas. Reg. § 1.704-3 |
| 3 | Excess nonrecourse liability — partner's share of partnership profits | Treas. Reg. § 1.752-3(a)(3) |

Pinned by `nonrecourse_tier1_only_minimum_gain`, `nonrecourse_tier2_only_704c_gain`, `nonrecourse_tier3_only_excess`, `nonrecourse_all_three_tiers_summed` ($5K + $3K + $2K = $10K), `nonrecourse_allocation_when_no_erol`, and `three_tier_nonrecourse_sum_invariant` (4-cell tier-combination sweep).

**Multi-regime invariants** — `liability_change_directly_translates_to_basis_change_invariant` (4-cell liability-change sweep with basis directly tracking ±change); `deemed_contribution_and_distribution_mutually_exclusive_invariant` (3-cell sweep — never both positive simultaneously); `section_731_gain_only_when_distribution_exceeds_basis_invariant` (4-cell basis × decrease sweep); `three_tier_nonrecourse_sum_invariant`; `single_transaction_netting_note_present` (Treas. Reg. § 1.752-1 netting rule UX regression).

**Sibling partnership cluster note** — every result references § 721 (contribution non-recognition — partner's initial outside basis), § 731 (distribution gain/loss — § 752(b) feeds § 731(a)(1) gain), § 704(b) (substantial economic effect — minimum gain framework for tier 1), § 704(c) (built-in gain allocation — tier 2), § 704(d) (basis-limited loss using § 752-adjusted basis), § 705 (partner basis adjustments aggregating § 752 + § 704 + distributions), and § 752 structural centrality — liability allocation feeds every other partnership computation. Pinned by `sibling_cluster_note_present`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-752`. Twenty-one tests pin: **liability increase treated as contribution** (basis +$50K); **liability zero change no deemed event**; **liability decrease treated as distribution within basis** ($170K new basis); **liability decrease exceeds basis triggers § 731 gain** ($50K gain on $50K basis with $100K decrease); **liability decrease exactly equals basis no gain** (boundary); **nonrecourse tier 1 only minimum gain**; **nonrecourse tier 2 only § 704(c) gain**; **nonrecourse tier 3 only excess**; **nonrecourse all three tiers summed**; **recourse allocation when bears EROL** (Treas. Reg. § 1.752-2); **nonrecourse allocation when no EROL** (3-tier note); **single transaction netting note present** (Treas. Reg. § 1.752-1); **liability change directly translates to basis change invariant** (4-cell sweep with $50K basis floor to avoid § 731 gain); **deemed contribution and distribution mutually exclusive invariant** (3-cell sweep); **§ 731 gain only when distribution exceeds basis invariant** (4-cell basis × decrease sweep); **three-tier nonrecourse sum invariant** (4-cell tier combinations); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 752 + (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) + § 1.752-1/-2/-3 + § 1.704-2/-3 + TD 10014 + December 2, 2024 + § 721 + § 731 + § 704(b)/(c)/(d) + § 705); **sibling cluster note present** (UX-text regression for partnership cluster + § 752 structural centrality); **defensive negative basis clamped**; **defensive negative tier clamped**; **extreme amounts no overflow** ($1B liability → $1B basis via saturating math).

`traderview-expense::section_6694` is the **IRC § 6694 tax return preparer penalty module** — direct preparer liability for understatements of taxpayer liability, layered on top of taxpayer-side penalties under § 6662 / § 6662A / § 6707A. Trader-critical because trader-CPAs commonly take aggressive positions on Schedule D, mark-to-market § 475(f) elections, wash-sale aggregation under § 1091, and partnership K-1 allocations. Sibling to the preparer + promoter penalty cluster: § 6695 (preparer information return penalties), § 6700 (promoter penalties), § 6701 (aiding and abetting understatement).

**§ 6694 two-tier penalty structure**:

| Subsection | Trigger | Penalty | Reasonable cause exception |
|------------|---------|---------|----------------------------|
| § 6694(a) | Understatement due to unreasonable position | Greater of $1,000 OR 50% of preparer fee | § 6694(a)(3) — yes, if reasonable cause + good faith |
| § 6694(b) | Willful understatement OR reckless/intentional disregard | Greater of $5,000 OR 75% of preparer fee | None — no exception available |

**§ 6694(a)(2) — three unreasonable-position paths** (any one engages):

| Prong | Statute | Trigger |
|-------|---------|---------|
| (A) | § 6694(a)(2)(A) | Position NOT disclosed + NO substantial authority |
| (B) | § 6694(a)(2)(B) | Position DISCLOSED + NO reasonable basis |
| (C) | § 6694(a)(2)(C) | Tax shelter / § 6662A reportable transaction + NOT more-likely-than-not |

Standards of authority (per Treasury regulations):
- **Substantial authority**: ~35-40% probability of sustaining the position
- **Reasonable basis**: ≥ 20% probability (lower than substantial authority)
- **More likely than not**: > 50% probability (required for shelter/reportable cases)

Pinned by `three_unreasonable_position_paths_truth_table` (4-cell sweep — baseline + 3 paths), `undisclosed_with_substantial_authority_no_penalty`, `undisclosed_without_substantial_authority_penalty`, `disclosed_with_reasonable_basis_no_penalty`, `disclosed_without_reasonable_basis_penalty`, `tax_shelter_more_likely_than_not_no_penalty`, `tax_shelter_without_more_likely_than_not_penalty`, and `shelter_supersedes_substantial_authority_invariant` (even with substantial authority, shelter requires more-likely-than-not).

**§ 6694(a)(3) reasonable-cause + good-faith exception**. No § 6694(a) penalty if it is shown that, considering all facts and circumstances, the understatement was due to reasonable cause AND the preparer acted in good faith. Note: this exception is NOT available for § 6694(b) penalties. Pinned by `reasonable_cause_excuses_6694a`.

**§ 6694(a) penalty math** — greater of $1,000 floor OR 50% of preparer fee. Low-fee preparers hit the floor; high-fee preparers pay percentage. Pinned by `fee_based_50_percent_exceeds_minimum` ($10K fee → $5K penalty) and `fee_based_50_percent_low_fee_floor_applies` ($500 fee × 50% = $250 < $1K floor → floor wins).

**§ 6694(b) willful or reckless conduct — no reasonable-cause exception**. Higher floor ($5,000) and higher percentage (75%). § 6694(b)(2)(A) — willful understatement; § 6694(b)(2)(B) — reckless or intentional disregard of rules/regulations. Pinned by `willful_understatement_engages_6694b`, `reckless_disregard_engages_6694b`, `no_understatement_no_6694b_even_if_willful` (must be an actual understatement), `fee_based_75_percent_exceeds_minimum` ($10K fee → $7,500 penalty), and `fee_based_75_percent_low_fee_floor_applies` (floor $5K wins on $1K fee).

**§ 6694(b)(3) no-stacking coordination**. When both (a) and (b) trigger, total penalty = max of the two (not sum). § 6694(b)(3) explicitly reduces (b) by any (a) already imposed on the same return. Pinned by `both_a_and_b_trigger_no_stacking` ($100K fee → (a) = $50K, (b) = $75K, total = $75K), `only_a_no_stacking_total_equals_a`, and `only_b_no_stacking_total_equals_b`.

**Multi-regime invariants** — `b_penalty_strictly_higher_than_a_invariant` ($5K = 5× $1K floor; 75% = 1.5× 50%); `penalty_constants_invariant` (all four constants verified); `three_unreasonable_position_paths_truth_table` (4-cell exhaustive sweep over the three (a) paths plus baseline).

**Sibling cluster note** — every result references § 6695 (preparer info-return penalties), § 6700 (promoter penalties), § 6701 (aiding/abetting), § 6662 (taxpayer accuracy), § 6662A (reportable-transaction-understatement), § 6707A (disclosure failure), § 7701(a)(36) (preparer definition), plus trader-specific position categories (§ 475(f), § 1091, partnership K-1). Pinned by `sibling_cluster_note_present` (UX-text regression for 7-statute cluster + trader-position references).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6694`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **no understatement no penalty**; **undisclosed with substantial authority no penalty**; **undisclosed without substantial authority penalty**; **disclosed with reasonable basis no penalty**; **disclosed without reasonable basis penalty**; **tax shelter more-likely-than-not no penalty**; **tax shelter without more-likely-than-not penalty**; **reasonable cause excuses § 6694(a)**; **fee-based 50% exceeds minimum** ($5K on $10K fee); **fee-based 50% low fee floor applies** ($1K floor on $500 fee); **willful understatement engages § 6694(b)**; **reckless disregard engages § 6694(b)**; **no understatement no § 6694(b) even if willful**; **fee-based 75% exceeds minimum** ($7,500 on $10K fee); **fee-based 75% low fee floor applies** ($5K floor on $1K fee); **both (a) and (b) trigger no stacking** ($75K total on $100K fee); **only (a) no stacking total equals (a)**; **only (b) no stacking total equals (b)**; **(b) penalty strictly higher than (a) invariant** (5× floor; 1.5× percentage); **three unreasonable-position paths truth table** (4-cell sweep — baseline + 3 paths); **shelter supersedes substantial authority invariant**; **penalty constants invariant** ($1K / 50% / $5K / 75%); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 6694 + § 6694(a) + (a)(2)(A)/(B)/(C) + (a)(3) + (b) + (b)(2)(A)/(B) + (b)(3) + § 1.6694-1/-2/-3 + § 6662(d)(2)(B) + § 6662A + § 7701(a)(36)); **sibling cluster note present** (UX-text regression for § 6695 + § 6700 + § 6701 + § 6662 + § 6662A + § 6707A + § 475(f) + § 1091 trader positions); **defensive negative preparer fee clamped**; **zero fee floor still applies** (floor $1K wins on $0 fee); **defensive huge fee no overflow** ($100M fee → $50M penalty via saturating math).

`traderview-expense::section_6695` is the **IRC § 6695 preparer information return penalties module** — direct sibling to `section_6694` (preparer substantive position penalty, iter 254). Where § 6694 punishes UNREASONABLE POSITIONS on the return, § 6695 punishes PROCEDURAL FAILURES by the preparer — failure to sign, failure to furnish copy to taxpayer, failure to retain records, failure to exercise due diligence on credit eligibility determinations. Together § 6694 + § 6695 cover both substantive and procedural sides of preparer liability. 2025 amounts per Rev. Proc. 2024-40 inflation adjustments.

**Per-failure penalties — $60 each (2025), max $31,500/year per subsection**:

| Subsection | Failure |
|------------|---------|
| § 6695(a) | Failure to furnish copy of return or claim to taxpayer |
| § 6695(b) | Failure to sign return or claim |
| § 6695(c) | Failure to furnish PTIN identifying number |
| § 6695(d) | Failure to retain copy or list (3-year retention) |
| § 6695(e) | Failure to file correct information returns (Forms 1099 series for preparer payments) |

**Higher-tier per-failure penalties — $635 each (2025)**:

| Subsection | Failure |
|------------|---------|
| § 6695(f) | Negotiation/endorsement of refund check by preparer (no annual cap) |
| § 6695(g) | Failure to exercise due diligence on EITC / CTC-ACTC-ODC / AOTC / HOH (4 independent categories; max combined per return = $2,540) |

**§ 6695(g) due diligence — Treas. Reg. § 1.6695-2(b) requires**:
1. Complete and submit **Form 8867** (Paid Preparer's Earned Income Credit Checklist);
2. Compute credit using applicable worksheet;
3. Knowledge requirement — preparer cannot know or have reason to know information is incorrect;
4. Retain records for **3 years**.

**§ 6695(g) four-category multiplier** — single return claiming all four (EITC + CTC + AOTC + HOH) generates up to $2,540 in due diligence penalties. Pinned by `all_four_categories_on_same_return_2540_max` (1 + 1 + 1 + 1 failures = $2,540), `single_eitc_due_diligence_failure_635`, `ctc_failure_only_engages_g`, `aotc_failure_only_engages_g`, and `hoh_failure_only_engages_g`.

**Per-failure penalties (a)-(e)** — $60 each up to annual cap of $31,500 per subsection. 525 failures × $60 = $31,500 exactly (cap-boundary); 1,000 failures × $60 = $60,000 → capped at $31,500. Each subsection caps INDEPENDENTLY. Pinned by `single_a_failure_60_dollars` through `single_e_failure_60_dollars` (5 subsection tests), `cap_525_a_failures_at_31500` (exact-cap boundary), `cap_1000_a_failures_at_max` (over-cap clamps), and `each_subsection_caps_independently` (3 × $31,500 = $94,500 across three subsections).

**§ 6695(f) refund check negotiation — no annual cap**. Unlike the per-failure subsections, the refund-check-negotiation penalty has no maximum cap. 100 refund checks negotiated × $635 = $63,500 (no clamping). Pinned by `single_refund_check_negotiation_635_dollars` and `refund_check_no_annual_cap`.

**Parameterized amounts for non-2025 years** — module accepts `per_failure_penalty_cents`, `annual_max_cap_cents`, and `higher_tier_penalty_cents` inputs to support any tax year. Pinned by `parameterized_2024_amounts` ($60/$30,000 2024) and `parameterized_2024_higher_tier` ($600 2024).

**Multi-regime invariants** — `higher_tier_exceeds_per_failure_invariant` ($635 = ~10× $60); `section_6695g_max_equals_4x_higher_tier_invariant` ($2,540 = 4 × $635); `five_per_failure_subsections_truth_table` (each of (a)-(e) generates $60 on single failure); `constants_2025_invariant` ($60 / $31,500 / $635 / $2,540 all verified).

**Sibling cluster note** — every result references § 6694 (preparer substantive position), § 6700 (promoter penalties), § 6701 (aiding and abetting), and taxpayer-side companions § 6662 + § 6662A + § 6707A. Pinned by `sibling_cluster_note_present` (UX-text regression for 7-statute cluster + Form 8867).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6695`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **no failures no penalty**; **single (a)/(b)/(c)/(d)/(e) failure $60** (5 subsection tests); **525 (a) failures at exact $31,500 cap**; **1000 (a) failures cap at $31,500 max**; **each subsection caps independently** (3 × $31,500); **single refund check negotiation $635**; **refund check no annual cap** (100 × $635 = $63,500); **single EITC due diligence failure $635**; **all four (g) categories on same return $2,540 max**; **CTC failure only engages (g)**; **AOTC failure only engages (g)**; **HOH failure only engages (g)**; **combined penalty sums all subsections** ($60 + $120 + $635 + $635 = $1,450); **parameterized 2024 amounts** ($60/$30,000 2024); **parameterized 2024 higher tier** ($600 2024); **higher tier exceeds per failure invariant** (~10× ratio); **§ 6695(g) max equals 4× higher tier invariant** ($2,540 = 4 × $635); **five per-failure subsections truth table** (each generates $60); **2025 constants invariant** ($60 / $31,500 / $635 / $2,540); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 6695 + (a)/(b)/(c)/(d)/(e)/(f)/(g) + § 1.6695-1/-2 + Form 8867 + Rev. Proc. 2024-40); **sibling cluster note present** (UX-text regression for § 6694 + § 6700 + § 6701 + § 6662 + § 6662A + § 6707A + Form 8867); **defensive negative counts clamped**; **defensive negative per-failure clamped**; **extreme count no overflow** (100K × $635 = $63.5M via saturating math).

`traderview-expense::section_6700` is the **IRC § 6700 promoter penalty module — abusive tax shelter promotion**. Third member of the preparer + promoter penalty cluster after § 6694 (preparer substantive position, iter 254) and § 6695 (preparer procedural failures, iter 256). § 6700 targets the PROMOTER side — the organizer/seller of abusive tax shelter plans, not the preparer of the underlying return. Effective since January 1, 1990; substantially amended by the American Jobs Creation Act of 2004 (AJCA), Pub. L. 108-357 § 818, which raised the false-statement penalty from $1,000 to 50% of gross income.

**§ 6700(a) two-prong liability structure**:

| Prong | Statute | Element |
|-------|---------|---------|
| 1 | § 6700(a)(1) | Person organizes, assists in organizing, or participates in the sale of an interest in a partnership, investment plan, arrangement, or other entity |
| 2(A) | § 6700(a)(2)(A) | Makes or furnishes a false or fraudulent statement about any material matter |
| 2(B) | § 6700(a)(2)(B) | Makes or furnishes a gross valuation overstatement |

Both Prong 1 AND one of Prong 2 must engage for penalty to attach. Penalty applies REGARDLESS of (a) participant reliance or (b) actual underreporting by participant — § 6700 punishes the conduct of promotion itself.

**§ 6700(a)(2)(A) false/fraudulent statement — 50% gross income penalty**. Requires SCIENTER (promoter knew or had reason to know statement was false). Penalty = 50% × gross income derived from the activity (no cap). Pinned by `false_statement_with_scienter_50_percent_penalty` (50% × $1M = $500K), `false_statement_without_scienter_no_penalty` (no scienter → no engagement), `false_statement_zero_gross_income_zero_penalty` (50% × 0 = 0 but still flagged as violation), and `scienter_required_for_false_statement_only_invariant` (multi-prong sweep).

**§ 6700(b)(1) gross valuation overstatement — definition + penalty**. Defined as: stated value EXCEEDS 200 PERCENT of correct value, AND value is directly related to amount of any deduction or credit allowable to participant. Penalty = $1,000 per activity, OR (if promoter establishes that 100% × gross income is LESSER) 100% × gross income. Effectively a $1,000 cap for high-revenue promoters; reduced floor for low-revenue. No scienter requirement. Pinned by `valuation_at_exactly_200_percent_not_engaged` (statute reads "EXCEEDS"), `valuation_one_cent_above_200_percent_engaged` (single-cent precision), `valuation_300_percent_engages_with_1000_floor` ($1K floor applies when gross income > $1K), `valuation_overstatement_low_gross_income_reduces_penalty` ($500 gross income < $1K → 100% × $500 = $500), `valuation_overstatement_no_direct_deduction_no_violation` (direct-relationship element required), and `valuation_threshold_boundary_truth_table` (5-cell sweep at 100/199.99/200/200.0001/500%).

**Penalty math — both prongs may stack**. § 6700(a) and § 6700(b) penalties are NOT mutually exclusive — both can apply to the same promotion activity if both false statement + scienter AND gross valuation overstatement + direct relationship are present. Total penalty = (a) + (b). Pinned by `both_prongs_penalties_sum` ($1M gross × 50% + $1K floor = $501K).

**Valuation overstatement ratio reporting** — result struct includes `valuation_overstatement_ratio_bps` for downstream display. Division-by-zero protection: if correct value is 0 but stated value > 0, ratio reported as `i64::MAX`. Pinned by `valuation_ratio_in_bps_correctly_reported` ($25K stated / $10K correct = 250% = 25,000 bps) and `valuation_ratio_zero_correct_max`.

**Engagement-regardless principle** — § 6700 penalty applies regardless of (a) whether participant relied on the plan or (b) whether participant actually underreported tax. Promoter conduct is independently sanctionable. Pinned by `engagement_regardless_of_participant_reliance_note` (UX-text regression confirms note present in result).

**Multi-regime invariants** — `promoter_status_required_for_both_prongs_invariant` (Prong 1 organize/sell prerequisite for both (a) and (b) violations); `scienter_required_for_false_statement_only_invariant` (scienter element only on (a)(2)(A); (b)(1) doesn't require scienter); `valuation_threshold_boundary_truth_table` (5-cell precision sweep at 200% boundary); `penalty_constants_invariant` (50% / $1,000 / 200% constants).

**Sibling cluster note** — every result references the full preparer + promoter penalty cluster: § 6694 (preparer substantive position), § 6695 (preparer procedural failures), § 6701 (aiding and abetting understatement), § 6011 (taxpayer Form 8886), § 6662 (accuracy), § 6662A (reportable-transaction-understatement), § 6707A (taxpayer disclosure failure), and § 7408 (injunction remedy — operates as the principal enforcement arsenal alongside § 6700 + § 6701). Pinned by `sibling_cluster_note_present` (UX-text regression for 8-statute cluster).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6700`. Twenty-four tests pin: **no promoter status no penalty**; **promoter status alone no penalty**; **false statement with scienter 50% penalty** ($500K on $1M gross); **false statement without scienter no penalty**; **false statement zero gross income zero penalty**; **valuation at exactly 200% not engaged** (statute reads "exceeds"); **valuation one cent above 200% engaged**; **valuation 300% engages with $1,000 floor**; **valuation overstatement low gross income reduces penalty** ($500 < $1K floor → $500); **valuation overstatement no direct deduction no violation**; **both prongs penalties sum** ($500K + $1K); **valuation ratio in BPS correctly reported** (250% = 25,000 bps); **valuation ratio zero correct = MAX** (division-by-zero protection); **promoter status required for both prongs invariant**; **scienter required for false statement only invariant**; **valuation threshold boundary truth table** (5-cell sweep at 100/199.99/200/200.0001/500%); **penalty constants invariant** (50% / $1,000 / 200%); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 6700 + (a)(1) + (a)(2)(A) + (a)(2)(B) + (a) flush + (b)(1) + Pub. L. 108-357 § 818 + October 22, 2004 + Form 14242 + § 7408); **sibling cluster note present** (UX-text regression for § 6694 + § 6695 + § 6701 + § 6011 + § 6662 + § 6662A + § 6707A + § 7408); **defensive negative gross income clamped**; **defensive negative valuation clamped**; **defensive extreme gross income no overflow** ($1B × 50% = $500M via saturating math); **scienter note when statement made without knowledge**; **engagement regardless of participant reliance note** (UX-text regression).

`traderview-expense::section_6701` is the **IRC § 6701 aiding-and-abetting penalty module** — fourth and final member of the preparer + promoter penalty cluster after § 6694 (preparer substantive position, iter 254), § 6695 (preparer procedural failures, iter 256), and § 6700 (promoter penalties, iter 258). § 6701 captures the BROADEST range of conduct in the cluster — any person who aids, assists, procures, or advises with respect to preparation of a document they KNOW would result in another person's tax understatement, regardless of preparer or promoter status. Per IRS Chief Counsel Memorandum 201531015, penalties are assessed per-document (not per-affected-taxpayer), but the § 6701(b)(2) one-per-taxpayer-per-period rule limits geometric growth.

**§ 6701(a) three-element test** (all required):

| Element | Statute | Test |
|---------|---------|------|
| (1) | § 6701(a)(1) | Person aids/assists/procures/advises with respect to preparation or presentation of any portion of a return, affidavit, claim, or other document |
| (2) | § 6701(a)(2) | Person knows or has reason to believe portion will be used in connection with any material matter arising under internal revenue laws |
| (3) | § 6701(a)(3) | Person knows portion (if so used) would result in understatement of liability for tax of ANOTHER person |

All three required. Pinned by `no_aid_no_engagement`, `aid_without_material_matter_knowledge_no_engagement`, `aid_without_understatement_knowledge_no_engagement`, and `three_element_truth_table` (8-cell exhaustive sweep — only the all-three-true cell engages).

**§ 6701(b)(1) penalty rates**:

| Subsection | Taxpayer type | Per-document penalty |
|------------|---------------|----------------------|
| § 6701(b)(1)(A) | Non-corporate (individual, partnership, trust, estate) | $1,000 |
| § 6701(b)(1)(B) | **Corporate** | **$10,000** (10× non-corporate rate) |

Pinned by `all_three_elements_engages_non_corporate_1000`, `all_three_elements_engages_corporate_10000`, `corporate_rate_10x_noncorporate_invariant`, `corporate_taxpayer_uses_higher_rate`, and `non_corporate_uses_lower_rate`.

**§ 6701(b)(2) one-per-taxpayer-per-period rule**. If a person is subject to a § 6701(a) penalty for any document relating to a particular taxpayer for a particular taxable period, that person shall NOT be subject to additional § 6701(a) penalties with respect to OTHER documents relating to the SAME taxpayer for the SAME period. Limits geometric penalty growth. Computed as `min(documents, distinct_taxpayers)` when all documents share the same period; spans of multiple periods bypass the cap. Pinned by `ten_documents_same_taxpayer_one_period_one_penalty` (10 docs → 1 penalty), `ten_documents_ten_taxpayers_ten_penalties` (1:1 ratio → 10 penalties × $1K = $10K), `ten_documents_ten_taxpayers_corporate_100k` (corporate × 10 × $10K = $100K), `five_documents_three_taxpayers_three_penalties` (min(5, 3) = 3), and `multi_period_documents_each_count` (multi-period bypasses cap).

**§ 6701(f) coordination — § 6701 supersedes § 6694**. When § 6701(a) engages on a document, § 6694(a)/(b) preparer penalties may NOT be assessed on that same document. § 6701 is the BROADER and MORE SERIOUS liability (knowledge of understatement is required; vs. § 6694's unreasonable-position or willful/reckless conduct). Pinned by `supersedes_section_6694_when_engaged` and `does_not_supersede_when_not_engaged`.

**Multi-regime invariants** — `three_element_truth_table` (8-cell sweep — only `(true, true, true)` engages); `corporate_rate_10x_noncorporate_invariant` ($10K = 10 × $1K); `penalty_constants_invariant` ($1,000 / $10,000 confirmed).

**Sibling cluster note** — every result references the full 4-statute preparer + promoter cluster (§ 6694 + § 6695 + § 6700 + § 6701), taxpayer-side companions (§ 6011 + § 6662 + § 6662A + § 6707A), and § 7408 injunction remedy for § 6700 + § 6701 conduct. Pinned by `sibling_cluster_note_present` (UX-text regression for full cluster + § 6701-broadest-member note).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6701`. Twenty-four tests pin: **no aid no engagement**; **aid without material matter knowledge no engagement**; **aid without understatement knowledge no engagement**; **all three elements engages non-corporate $1,000**; **all three elements engages corporate $10,000**; **ten documents same taxpayer one period one penalty** (one-per-taxpayer rule); **ten documents ten taxpayers ten penalties** (1:1 ratio); **ten documents ten taxpayers corporate $100K**; **five documents three taxpayers three penalties** (min math); **multi-period documents each count** (bypasses one-per-taxpayer cap); **supersedes § 6694 when engaged** (§ 6701(f)); **does not supersede when not engaged**; **corporate rate 10× non-corporate invariant**; **three-element truth table** (8-cell sweep — only all-three-true engages); **penalty constants invariant** ($1K / $10K); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 6701 + (a)(1)/(a)(2)/(a)(3) + (b)(1)(A)/(b)(1)(B) + (b)(2) + (f) + § 6700 + § 7408 + IRM 20.1.6 + CCM 201531015); **sibling cluster note present** (UX-text regression for § 6694 + § 6695 + § 6700 + § 6701 + § 6011 + § 6662 + § 6662A + § 6707A + § 7408 + BROADEST member note); **defensive negative documents clamped**; **defensive negative taxpayers clamped to floor at one**; **extreme documents no overflow** (100K × $10K = $1B via saturating math); **no engagement no supersession**; **missing element specific notes** (three-element-specific note coverage).

`traderview-expense::section_1374` is the **IRC §1374 S-corporation built-in gains (BIG) tax module** — the integrity tax that prevents a C-corp from escaping corporate-level tax on pre-conversion appreciation by simply electing S-corp status. When a C-corp converts, its built-in gains remain exposed to corporate-level tax for a **5-year recognition period** under § 1374(d)(7), permanently set by the **PATH Act of 2015** (down from 10 years originally, then 7 years, then 5). The tax rate is the highest §11(b) corporate rate — **21% post-TCJA** — applied to net recognized built-in gain ([Beancount.io §1374 guide](https://beancount.io/blog/2026/05/10/section-1374-built-in-gains-tax-c-corp-s-corp-conversion-five-year-recognition-period-guide), [Cornell LII 26 U.S.C. § 1374](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/26/1374)).

**NUBIG (Net Unrealized Built-In Gain) at conversion** is the LIFETIME ceiling on what can ever be taxed under §1374:

```text
NUBIG = Σ(FMV − adjusted basis) at conversion
      − liabilities and deductible items at conversion
```

If NUBIG is $2M at conversion, the cumulative §1374 tax base over the entire 5-year recognition period can never exceed $2M — no matter how much gain is recognized in any single year. Pinned by `nubig_ceiling_binds_when_prior_years_exhausted_most_of_it`.

**NRBIG (Net Recognized Built-In Gain) for each year** = LESSER OF three limits per § 1374(d)(2):

1. **Recognized BIG limit**: (gross recognized built-in gain − recognized built-in loss) for the year
2. **Taxable income limit** (§ 1374(d)(2)(A)(ii)): the corporation's taxable income computed as if it were a C-corp, per § 1375(b)(1)(B)
3. **NUBIG ceiling**: conversion-date NUBIG MINUS cumulative prior-year NRBIG

When the **taxable income limit binds**, the excess recognized BIG **carries forward** within the recognition period under § 1374(d)(2)(B) and is treated as recognized BIG in subsequent years — surfaced via `nrbig_carryforward_for_subsequent_year` so callers can chain across the 5 years. This creates a planning incentive to bunch deductions into years when BIG is recognized: "if the corporation's taxable income can be reduced to zero, no Built-In-Gains Tax will be imposed" ([Foster Garvey — Subchapter S Part 1](https://www.foster.com/larry-s-tax-law/subchapter-s-part-1-the-built-in-gains-tax)).

**Two C-corp tax-attribute carryovers reduce BIG tax**:
- **§ 1374(b)(2) C-corp NOL deduction** — pre-conversion NOL carryforwards deductible against NRBIG; pinned by `c_corp_nol_reduces_taxable_nrbig` + `c_corp_nol_exceeds_nrbig_clamps_to_zero_tax`.
- **§ 1374(b)(3) credit offset** — pre-conversion general business credits, minimum tax credits, foreign tax credits reduce the BIG tax LIABILITY dollar-for-dollar (after rate applied); pinned by `credit_offset_reduces_tax_dollar_for_dollar` + `credit_offset_exceeds_tax_clamps_to_zero`.

**Outside the 5-year window**: BIG tax exposure ends. Returned via `in_recognition_period: false` + `binding_limit: OutsideRecognitionPeriod`. Pinned by `outside_5_year_recognition_period_no_tax`; boundary case `year_5_exact_boundary_still_inside` pins that year 5 (index 4 of the 5-year window) is still INSIDE.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-1374`.
